fresh_facts.claim: US Navy destroyers USS Michael Murphy and USS Frankie Eie Peterson Jr. attempted to enter the Strait of Hormuz on April 11, with Iranian media claiming IRGC forces locked missiles and drones on them and issued a 30-minute ultimatum.
US CENTCOM denied any confrontation in the Strait of Hormuz, stating ships transited freely.
President Trump announced on April 13 that the US will blockade the Strait of Hormuz to increase pressure on Iran after ceasefire talks failed.
The US has elite troops nearby including the 82nd Airborne and two Marine combat groups totaling 5,000-6,000 personnel, though not yet deployed.
Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu claimed on April 11 that recent operations destroyed Iran's heavy water plant at Arak, centrifuge array, and uranium production plant.
Israel continues strikes against Hezbollah in Lebanon, hitting over 200 targets in the past 24 hours to support ground operations up to the Litani River.
IRGC released footage on April 12 of forces warning a US destroyer in the Strait of Hormuz to change course or face attack.
IRGC Quds Force Commander Esmail Qa'ani stated on April 12 that the Axis of Resistance remains strong and ready for confrontation.
US-Iran ceasefire talks in Islamabad collapsed on April 12 after 21 hours without reaching a breakthrough.
Pakistan's PM Shehbaz met both US and Iranian delegations, calling talks a stepping stone toward durable peace.
Iran contacts Lebanon for ceasefire implementation; Macron and Saudi Crown Prince discussed expanding ceasefire to Lebanon.
Iranian Deputy FM Gharibabadi stated Iran is prepared for any scenario and claims US defeats strengthen Iran's position.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed in US-Israel strikes on February 28, 2026, along with senior officials.
An Interim Leadership Council consisting of President Pezeshkian, Chief Justice Mohseni-Eje'i, and Guardian Council rep. Arafi holds temporary power following Khamenei's death.
The Assembly of Experts seeks a successor to the Supreme Leader with no clear frontrunner or timeline announced.
Reports claim the new or interim supreme leader, possibly Mojtaba Khamenei, has severe disfiguring wounds and is rarely seen.
The Iranian regime detained tens of thousands following US-Israel strikes; state reports approximately 3,000 deaths while rights groups cite significantly higher figures.
fresh_facts.source: Human rights organizations and Iranian state

fresh_facts.claim: Recent protests in Iran are described as the most serious unrest ever, threatening the regime's character and legitimacy.
Trump threatened Iran with fire and fury while empty oil tankers head to the US, flaunting reserves.
France and Turkey agreed that any ceasefire must include Lebanon.
French lawmakers called for united Iranian opposition for democratic transition.
John Kerry stated that Netanyahu lobbied for Iran strike for decades.
The US revoked residency of three Iranians over regime links during ongoing negotiations.
CSIS analysis notes that a fragile ceasefire hides unresolved nuclear ambitions, Lebanon destabilization, and rising terrorism.
A former CIA official stated that the worst outcome is the Islamic Republic's survival.

regime_fall.current_status: Following the collapse of Islamabad peace talks on April 12, the Trump administration has announced a comprehensive naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz with massive military deployments, while Iran's interim leadership—following Supreme Leader Khamenei's death in February—faces unprecedented internal unrest, succession uncertainty, and coordinated military pressure from the US and Israel.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Trump announced on April 13 a comprehensive US naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz to increase pressure on Iran following the failed ceasefire talks, with elite combat forces including the 82nd Airborne and two Marine combat groups positioned for potential deployment.
